name: Savory Rice-Stuffed Peppers
recipeIngredient: 4 slices lowfat mozzarella cheese, thin, part-skimmed
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine the broth, brown rice and wild rice in a medium saucepan. Bring to a boil, cover, reduce heat and simmer until the broth has been absorbed, 10 minutes. Set aside.
2. Slice the tops off the peppers and scoop out the insides, cutting the white membranes out. Wrap each pepper in waxed paper and microwave on High for approximately 3 minutes (depends on individual microwave).
3. Meanwhile, sauté the onions and garlic in the oil in a large skillet until tender, about 5 minutes. Add peas, corn and carrots. Cover and cook over low heat for about 5 minutes.
4. Stir in the rice, shredded mozzarella and oregano.
5. Coat a deep 3 quart casserole with vegetable cooking spray. Fill the peppers with rice mixture; place in the dish. Top each with a slice of mozzarella.
6. Cover the casserole and bake at 350°F for 15 minutes. Remove the lid and bake an additional 5 minutes longer. Serve with warmed tomato sauce. Serves 4 (Variation: Omit wild rice and increase to 1 cup brown rice)
